name: Generate and Publish WordPress Blog Posts with AI

parameters.content:
## Generate a draft SEO-optimised article on your Wordpress site

### How it works

Send a brief via webhook and Claude AI writes the article, Imagen 4 generates a featured image, a WordPress draft is created with Rank Math SEO set and a Slack notification is sent on completion with a link to the draft for review.

**Activate the workflow** then POST to `/webhook/article-publisher`:
```
{ "prompt": "Write an article about..." }
```
Optional body fields: `category_id`, `tone_guide`, `style_guide`

### Setup

**1. Update the CONFIG node** with your:
- `WORDPRESS_URL` — your WordPress site URL
- `DEFAULT_CATEGORY_ID` — default category ID for new posts 
- `AUTHOR_ID` — WordPress user ID to assign as post author
- `SLACK_CHANNEL` — your Slack channel ID (e.g. C0123456789)
- `TONE_GUIDE` — your writing voice and persona (overridable per request)
- `STYLE_GUIDE` — your article structure preferences (overridable per request)
- `IMAGE_STYLE_PROMPT` — style prefix (prepended to every image prompt if image generation is enabled)
- `WEBHOOK_SECRET` — optional Bearer token to secure the endpoint (leave blank to disable)

**2. Link credentials** in every HTTP Request node:
- Anthropic API → GENERATE ARTICLE CONTENT
- Google AI (PaLM) API → GENERATE IMAGE
- WordPress API → UPLOAD IMAGE, SET IMAGE ALT TEXT, UPSERT TAG, CREATE POST, SET RANK MATH SEO
- Slack OAuth2 → SLACK NOTIFICATION

### Customization
**(Optional) Rank Math SEO:** Install the mu-plugin on your WordPress site to allow writing SEO fields via the REST API. See template docs.

### Image Generation (optional)

Set `ENABLE_IMAGE_GENERATION` to `false` in CONFIG to skip.

When enabled, Google Imagen 4 generates a 16:9 featured image.
The filename and alt text are derived from the focus keyword.

Requires a Google AI (PaLM) API credential with Imagen 4 access.

### Rank Math SEO (optional)

Sets focus keyword, SEO title, and meta description via the WordPress REST API.

Requires the Rank Math plugin plus a custom mu-plugin to expose
the `rank_math_*` meta fields to the REST API.

Place `rank-math-rest.php` in `/wp-content/mu-plugins/`.
Full plugin code is in the template documentation.

assignments.name: IMAGE_STYLE_PROMPT
assignments.value: Flat design illustration, clean modern lines, no text, blog post featured image displayed full width at top in landscape orientation. Subject:

assignments.name: TONE_GUIDE
assignments.value:
Write in first person as [Your Name/Persona]. [Describe your background briefly].

Voice: [Describe your preferred tone — e.g. direct and warm, no hype, no filler openers]. Short declarative sentences mixed with longer ones for rhythm.

Frame content as: here is what I tried, here is what I found, here is how you might apply it. Acknowledge trade-offs honestly.

Reader: [Describe your target audience — e.g. semi-technical professionals running their own projects].

[Your site/brand name]: [Brief description of your site purpose and angle].

assignments.name: STYLE_GUIDE
assignments.value:
Structure every article as follows:
- Opening paragraph: start with an observation or friction point.
- H2 sections for main topics, H3 for subsections where needed.
- Use bullet lists sparingly — only for genuine lists of 3+ parallel items. Never use bullets to avoid writing proper sentences.
- Tables for comparisons, not bullets.
- Closing paragraph: one concrete takeaway or honest assessment. No "In conclusion" or summary restatement.
- Do not use bold text for decoration. Bold only for genuinely critical terms or warnings.
- Do not end sections with transition phrases like "Lets explore..." or "Read on to find out...".
- Ideal length: 800-1400 words for most articles. Go longer only if the topic genuinely requires it.

assignments.name: claude_prompt
assignments.value:
=You are a content writer.

TONE GUIDE:
{{ $json.tone_guide }}

STYLE GUIDE:
{{ $json.style_guide }}

Write an article based on this brief: {{ $json.prompt }}

Requirements:
- Follow the tone guide and style guide precisely
- SEO title under 60 characters
- Meta description under 155 characters
- Tags should reference only tools and concepts actually discussed in the article

Return ONLY a valid JSON object (no markdown, no code fences), with these exact fields:
{
  "title": "...",
  "content": "...full HTML article body using h2, p, ul/li tags...",
  "excerpt": "...two sentence summary...",
  "tags": ["...", "..."],
  "seo_title": "...under 60 chars...",
  "seo_description": "...under 155 chars...",
  "focus_keyword": "...single most important keyword phrase...",
  "image_subject": "...one sentence describing a visual scene for the featured image...",
  "affiliate_flag": "...note tools where affiliate links would be natural, or false if none..."
}

parameters.jsCode:
const tags = $('PARSE ARTICLE').item.json.tags || [];
return tags.map(tag => ({ json: { tag_name: tag } }));

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ { "title": $("PARSE ARTICLE").item.json.title, "content": $("PARSE ARTICLE").item.json.content, "excerpt": $("PARSE ARTICLE").item.json.excerpt, "status": "draft", "categories": [Number($("PREPARE INPUT").item.json.category_id)], "tags": $("COLLECT TAG IDS").item.json.tag_ids, "featured_media": $("MERGE IMAGE RESULT").item.json.media_id, "author": Number($("CONFIG").item.json.AUTHOR_ID) } }}
